free_text: Activation cross sections of alpha particle-induced
reactions on natural hafnium up to 50 MeV
Natural high purity metallic foils of Hf and Ti for
consisting of Hf-HF and Ti-Ti foil pairs were
irradiated with alpha beam of E=51. Beam energy was
determined by TOF method. Irradiation was done in a
The beam intensity was monitored and registered in
Six series of spectra were recorded with different
cooling times to be able to follow the decay.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT, Lun/LBNL and
IAEA-NDS Live Chart online data libraries.
Correction for attenuation of low energy gamma photons
in tha Hf target foils was done.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primari incident beam was
+- 0.2MeV which increased up to +-1.5 MeV in the last
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
